name: Sudan
headline: Sudan FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — May 17 cycle sustains May 15-16 WFP/FAO/UNICEF joint famine confirmation (19.5M acute food insecurity; Al Fasher and Kadugli famine-confirmed; 20 additional risk areas). Additional carryover from May 2026 reporting cycle: UN Fact-Finding Mission (February 2026) concluded RSF committed crimes against humanitarian, war crimes, and genocide against non-Arab communities in El Fasher (140+ victim interviews); Just Security (May 2026) report on starvation crimes in Darfur with UN FFM concluding RSF used starvation as method of warfare (war crime). ICC warning on RSF war crimes active. Cumulative death toll: 270,000+ likely killed by violence, starvation, and disease total. 880 civilians killed by drones in first 4 months of 2026; drones now 80%+ of civilian casualties. Aid appeal still only 16% funded. UN OHCHR 'high alert' active. Both RSF and SAF parties obstruct humanitarian access. Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change. Documentation expansion: UN FFM genocide finding is highest-tier external determination evidence; this is the canonical formal-genocide-determination methodology marker.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. UN OHCHR 'high alert' active; UN agencies' documentation infrastructure operational; state-level (both RSF and SAF) awareness present but conduct is the aid-obstruction itself.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 19.5M acute food insecurity; famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli; 800,000+ children at risk of severe malnutrition. Mass killing at Al-Rashid university dormitory in El Fasher (UN FFM).

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC2 proportionality: 19.5M facing acute food insecurity with 16%-funded response. AC4 resource-mobilization failure compounded by dual-party aid-obstruction.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. UN FFM genocide finding against RSF specifically for conduct against non-Arab communities — definitional EQU-dimension floor signal.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. IHL on humanitarian access violated; UN FFM finding of starvation as method of warfare (war crime) is canonical IHL-violation category.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. UN FFM genocide finding is highest-tier external accountability determination; no actor self-accountability documented. ICC warning on RSF war crimes active.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. Famine confirmed + UN genocide finding + 270,000+ likely killed total = systems-level humanitarian collapse and genocide-determination.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. Dual-party conflict-actor posture not values-aligned; no I1 consistency-under-pressure signal.

evidence.summary: WFP/FAO/UNICEF joint news release May 15-16: 19.5 million people face acute food insecurity; famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli with risk in 20 additional areas across Greater Darfur and Greater Kordofan. 800,000+ children at risk of severe malnutrition.

UN Fact-Finding Mission (February 2026): RSF committed crimes against humanity, war crimes, and genocide against non-Arab communities in El Fasher based on 140+ victim interviews. Mass killing at Al-Rashid university dormitory.
Just Security (May 2026): UN FFM specifically concluded RSF used starvation as a method of warfare (war crime). Report offers new evidence of starvation crimes in Darfur.
NBC: ICC warning on RSF war crimes (February 2026). 270,000+ likely killed by violence, starvation, and disease total.
NPR April 15 2026: Sudan Darfur war genocide famine — comprehensive context coverage.
Global R2P Sudan country page: ongoing R2P watch designation; cumulative atrocities-documentation.

keyEvidence: WFP/FAO/UNICEF joint May 15-16 release: 19.5M acute food insecurity; famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli.
UN FFM February 2026: RSF committed genocide against non-Arab communities in El Fasher (140+ victim interviews).
Starvation as method of warfare confirmed as war crime by UN FFM.
ICC warning on RSF war crimes active; 270,000+ likely killed total.
880 civilians killed by drones in first 4 months 2026 (80%+ of civilian casualties).
Aid appeal 16% funded; both RSF and SAF obstruct humanitarian access.
Floor sustained at 0.0; UN genocide finding documented as highest-tier external accountability determination.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. The UN Fact-Finding Mission's formal genocide determination for RSF conduct in El Fasher is the methodology's canonical highest-tier external accountability evidence — a formal UN finding of genocide is the most authoritative determination available outside an ICC conviction. The WFP/FAO/UNICEF joint famine confirmation (Tier-5 humanitarian evidence per May 16 cycle) and the UN FFM starvation-as-warfare-as-war-crime finding compound the floor designation. Already at floor — no downward movement available. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: Famine expansion to the 20 additional risk areas
RSF and SAF conflict trajectory: any escalation
ICC engagement on RSF (war crimes warning + UN FFM genocide finding)
Drone-attributed casualty pattern: monitor for 1,000+ trigger
Mass-killing events at additional locations (Al-Rashid pattern)
carryForward: UN FFM genocide finding as formal-genocide-determination methodology marker
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: the UN Fact-Finding Mission's formal genocide determination (Sudan El Fasher, February 2026) is the canonical instance of the 'formal-genocide-determination' methodology marker — distinct from atrocity-documentation, war-crime-finding, and crime-against-humanity-finding. The methodology should formalize formal-genocide-determination as a Tier-5 evidence anchor under EQU-dimension scoring, given the legal-categorical weight of the determination. Sudan is the first floor-state in the country index to receive a formal UN genocide determination during the assessment window.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Floor sustained at 0.0.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
